summary: Full backend + frontend testing of the Swiggy/Zomato Aggregator command center demo. All 17 backend pytest tests passed (auth, demo seed verification, list/simulate/accept/reject/dispatch flows). End-to-end Playwright test of the OnlineOrders page passed all 17 steps: login -> redirect to / -> hero render -> 4 stat cards -> 8+ demo orders with all 8 expected customers -> status filters -> platform filters -> search -> simulate Swiggy/Zomato/burst+5 -> Accept/Reject/Dispatch -> Online sync indicator.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~820 lines and contains all routes, models, seeding, auth, and dashboard logic in one file. Per >700-line guideline, consider splitting into modules (auth.py, orders.py, online_orders.py, seed.py) for maintainability.

PATCH /api/tables/{tid}/status performs a nested await inside an update call ($set value computed via inline await) - works but is fragile and hard to read. Refactor to read-then-write. (Not exercised by current UI but still in codebase.)
POST /api/online-orders/simulate accepts an untyped `dict` body; should use a Pydantic model (e.g. SimulateIn { platform: Literal['swiggy','zomato'] | None }) for validation and OpenAPI clarity.
JWT secret is loaded from env (good) but cookie is set with secure=False; acceptable for preview but should be True in production.
Polling every 6s via setInterval refresh() in OnlineOrders.jsx — acceptable for demo, but websockets/SSE would be more idiomatic for a 'live inbox'. Not a blocker.
test_credentials: admin@pos.com / admin123 (admin), cashier@pos.com / cashier123 (cashier) - both verified working
seed_data_creation: Backend seed_defaults() seeds 8 demo Swiggy/Zomato orders (Aarav Khanna, Ishita Mehra, Rohit Sharma, Priya Raghavan, Vikram Joshi, Neha Patel, Karan Verma, Sneha Iyer) across incoming/accepted/dispatched/rejected statuses on first run - verified present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is stripped to just 2 routes (/login, /). OnlineOrders.jsx is the only protected page. All backend modules (menu, tables, KOT, dashboard) remain in server.py and are NOT exposed in UI by design - do not report as bugs. Auth uses both httpOnly cookie and Authorization: Bearer fallback. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_online_orders.py covers all critical paths.
mocked_apis_verified: Swiggy/Zomato simulate endpoint produces realistic orders with SWI-/ZOM- external_ids and selects random items from seeded menu. Confirmed working.
